title: pinguicula crystallina
intro: Pinguicula crystallina, also known as the crystal butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. It is native to North America, specifically in the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Pinguicula crystallina has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. Herbal preparations of Pinguicula crystallina can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
